description:
You are a database-reliability engineer who has just received an alert that several nightly backups have been intermittently failing.  
Your job is to comb through a small collection of text logs, extract every failure that happened during the most recent 7-day window (inclusive), and build a concise, comma-separated report that management’s dashboard can ingest.

System layout that already exists when you start:
1. A log directory that contains one plain-text file for every second day of the past week.  
   • Path: /home/user/db_backups/logs/  
   • Filenames follow the pattern backup_YYYY-MM-DD.log  
   • All log lines share the exact structure  
     [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] LEVEL  db=<DATABASE_NAME>  …additional key/value pairs…  
   • Only lines whose LEVEL is “ERROR” represent failed backups.  These failure lines *always* contain two additional key/value pairs:  
        code=<UPPERCASE_ERROR_CODE>  
        msg="<Free-form English message>"  

2. A writable output directory:  
   /home/user/db_backups/reports/  

Goal:
Generate a single CSV file named
/home/user/db_backups/reports/backup_failures_report.csv

The CSV **must** conform to all of the following rules (the automated grader will parse it exactly):
• It is UTF-8 text, plain ASCII characters only.  
• The very first line is the fixed header below (spelled exactly, no spaces after commas):  
  timestamp,database,error_code,message  
• Each subsequent line represents one ERROR entry that fell between **2023-07-08 and 2023-07-14 inclusive** (that is the fixed 7-day window for this exercise; do NOT rely on the live system clock).  
• Lines must be ordered chronologically by the timestamp that appeared at the start of each log line.  
• For every qualifying ERROR line, extract and write these four fields, preserving original order and spacing inside the timestamp:  
   1. timestamp  – the value inside the square brackets, without the brackets  
   2. database   – the value after “db=”, exactly as is  
   3. error_code – the value after “code=”  
   4. message    – the value contained between the quotation marks following “msg=” but *without* the quotes  

Example (header + two fabricated rows only):  
timestamp,database,error_code,message  
2023-05-22 01:33:44,hr,BKUP-042,Disk quota exceeded  

You will need to:
a) Search *all* .log files in /home/user/db_backups/logs/ in parallel or via a single piped command stream;  
b) Use a single regular expression (or a pipeline of tools) to isolate the ERROR lines;  
c) Further restrict results to the fixed seven-day window shown above;  
d) Properly parse and format each line’s data into the CSV layout;  
e) Sort the final rows in ascending timestamp order before writing the file;  
f) Save the finished CSV at the exact path given and ensure it ends with a single trailing newline.

No other files should be altered or created.  The task is complete when backup_failures_report.csv exists at the path above and matches the required format byte-for-byte (header + five data rows + trailing newline).
truth:
<!--
Pre-seeded files (already on disk when the agent begins)
-------------------------------------------------------
Directory: /home/user/db_backups/logs/

File: /home/user/db_backups/logs/backup_2023-07-08.log
----------------------------------------------------------------
[2023-07-08 03:03:12] INFO  db=customers  status=SUCCESS  duration=43s
[2023-07-08 03:04:01] ERROR db=customers  code=BKUP-013  msg="Could not open backup file"
[2023-07-08 03:05:09] INFO  db=customers  status=END   duration=44s

File: /home/user/db_backups/logs/backup_2023-07-10.log
----------------------------------------------------------------
[2023-07-10 01:00:12] INFO  db=orders  status=START
[2023-07-10 01:01:55] ERROR db=orders  code=BKUP-001  msg="Tablespace read error"
[2023-07-10 01:02:40] INFO  db=orders  status=END duration=99s

File: /home/user/db_backups/logs/backup_2023-07-12.log
----------------------------------------------------------------
[2023-07-12 04:17:07] INFO  db=inventory  status=START
[2023-07-12 04:18:00] WARN  db=inventory  message="Slow I/O detected"
[2023-07-12 04:19:22] ERROR db=inventory  code=BKUP-047  msg="Checksum mismatch"
[2023-07-12 04:20:00] INFO  db=inventory  status=END duration=173s

File: /home/user/db_backups/logs/backup_2023-07-14.log
----------------------------------------------------------------
[2023-07-14 02:55:02] INFO  db=analytics  status=START
[2023-07-14 02:56:34] ERROR db=analytics  code=BKUP-001  msg="Tablespace read error"
[2023-07-14 03:01:10] ERROR db=analytics  code=BKUP-099  msg="Destination full"
[2023-07-14 03:02:00] INFO  db=analytics  status=END duration=355s

Directory to write to:
-------------------------------------------------------
/home/user/db_backups/reports/  (exists and is writable)

Final artefact that the agent must create
-------------------------------------------------------
File: /home/user/db_backups/reports/backup_failures_report.csv
Exact byte-for-byte contents (including trailing newline):
----------------------------------------------------------------
timestamp,database,error_code,message
2023-07-08 03:04:01,customers,BKUP-013,Could not open backup file
2023-07-10 01:01:55,orders,BKUP-001,Tablespace read error
2023-07-12 04:19:22,inventory,BKUP-047,Checksum mismatch
2023-07-14 02:56:34,analytics,BKUP-001,Tablespace read error
2023-07-14 03:01:10,analytics,BKUP-099,Destination full

(There should be exactly 6 lines, including the header.)

Any deviation from this precise content will cause the automated test to fail.
-->
name: task_000000_f66b0f62
